Project 01 — Social Enterprise Acumen, UK · Jan–Apr 2025

Love Music Party
Campaign

A multi-format digital and print campaign promoting a free youth music event, part of a publicly funded community arts programme. Designed to reach young people aged 11–16 in Houghton-le-Spring with low prior engagement in arts activities. Supported by Arts Council England and Sunderland City Council.

Designed a full suite of event promotion materials — from large-format print posters to platform-specific social posts. Multiple distinct poster designs were released at timed intervals in the lead-up to the event, each with a different visual style and copy to maintain audience interest and build anticipation. In addition to campaign design, facilitated a live cross-cultural activity at the event itself.

50+Event attendees
6+Distinct formats
£200–500Campaign budget
3Phased releases
Canva
Meta Business Suite
Instagram · Facebook
Print & digital formats
Phased content rollout
Community engagement
Arts Council England
Sunderland City Council

📅  Phased release strategy: Multiple distinct poster designs were released at fixed intervals — an announcement poster, a mid-campaign reminder, and a final "this week" call-to-action — each adapting visual style and copy to sustain audience interest across different platforms and audience touchpoints.

↑ Print posters, Instagram (1080×1350px) and Facebook posts — each adapted per platform. The bold heart character with sunglasses featured as a recurring visual motif across social posts. Hover any image and click to view full size.

🎨

Cross-Cultural Activity: Warli Painting Stall

Ran a hands-on Warli art stall for children at the Love Music Party community event — introducing a traditional Indian folk art form as an interactive, educational experience. Designed to bring a cross-cultural element to the programme, making art accessible and engaging for young participants unfamiliar with the practice.

Live event Cross-cultural education Community engagement

Project 02 — Social Enterprise Acumen, UK · Jan–Apr 2025

Mystery Trail App
Launch Support

Supported the launch of a community heritage trail app for Houghton-le-Spring — conducting audience research and gap analysis, creating early-stage promotional visuals, and drafting outreach messaging for local partner organisations.

Early-stage promotional mock-ups were created to test visual directions and communicate the app concept to stakeholders before final assets were commissioned. The research component identified target user groups and engagement gaps to inform the app's launch strategy.

Audience Research
Gap Analysis
Promotional Mock-ups
Stakeholder Outreach
Canva

↑ Early-stage mock-ups for stakeholder review. App logo placeholder is intentional — created before final brand assets were confirmed.

Project 03 — Nagoya University Coffee Hour Club, Japan · 2024

Bilingual Event
Communications

Designed Japanese–English promotional materials for cultural events at Nagoya University's Coffee Hour Club — a cross-cultural student society for Japanese and international students.

01

All materials produced natively in both Japanese and English — culturally adapted for each audience, not simply translated.

02

Events coordinated for 40+ participants across Japanese and international student communities in Nagoya.

03

Created within a fully immersive Japanese-language environment during a year-abroad placement — genuine bilingual production, not a classroom exercise.
